User.title: User
User.description: Represents an authenticated user within the system, with specific roles and access levels for the embroidery workshop.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: Email address of the user, used for login and notifications.

role.description: Assigned role determining user permissions and access within the system.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

CustomerLead.title: CustomerLead
CustomerLead.description: Represents a potential customer lead generated from the public web quoter, before being converted into a confirmed order.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CustomerLead entity.

firstName.description: First name of the potential customer.

lastName.description: Last name of the potential customer.

email.description: Email address of the potential customer.

phone.description: Phone number of the potential customer.

companyName.description: Optional company name associated with the lead.

quoteConfiguration.description: JSON string containing the detailed configuration of the quote generated by the web quoter, including product, logo, position, quantity, and estimated price.

status.description: Current status of the lead in the sales funnel.

sellerId.description: Reference to the User (seller) assigned to follow up on this lead. (Relationship: User 1:N CustomerLead)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the lead was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the lead was last updated.

Customer.title: Customer
Customer.description: Represents a confirmed customer with whom orders are placed.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Customer entity.

companyName.description: Optional company name if the customer is a business.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the customer record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the customer record was last updated.

ProductType.title: ProductType
ProductType.description: Defines the different types of products available for embroidery (e.g., T-shirt, Cap, Bag), including their base pricing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ProductType entity.

description.description: Detailed description of the product type.

basePrice.description: Base price of a single unit of this product type.

unit.description: Unit of measure for the product (e.g., 'unidad').

imageSvgUrl.description: URL to an SVG image representing the product for visual mockups.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the product type was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the product type was last updated.

EmbroideryPosition.title: EmbroideryPosition
EmbroideryPosition.description: Defines the possible positions where an embroidery can be placed on a product (e.g., Chest, Sleeve, Back).

id.description: Unique identifier for the EmbroideryPosition entity.

name.description: Name of the embroidery position (e.g., 'Pecho', 'Manga Izquierda').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the embroidery position was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the embroidery position was last updated.

Logo.title: Logo
Logo.description: Represents a logo uploaded by a customer or one of the pre-defined example logos available in the system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Logo entity.

name.description: Name or description of the logo.

imageUrl.description: URL to the stored image file of the logo (e.g., SVG, PNG) for use in mockups and production.

isExample.description: Indicates if this is a pre-defined example logo (true) or a customer-uploaded logo (false).

customerId.description: Reference to the Customer who uploaded this logo, if applicable. Can be null for example logos. (Relationship: Customer 1:N Logo)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the logo was uploaded or created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the logo details were last updated.

Machine.title: Machine
Machine.description: Represents an industrial embroidery machine available in the workshop, including its status and maintenance information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Machine entity.

name.description: Name or identifier of the machine (e.g., 'Máquina A1', 'Bordadora #2').

status.description: Current operational status of the machine.

lastMaintenanceDate.description: Timestamp of the last maintenance performed on the machine.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the machine record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the machine record was last updated.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a customer's confirmed order for embroidery services, tracking its lifecycle from creation to delivery.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

customerId.description: Reference to the Customer who placed this order. (Relationship: Customer 1:N Order)

sellerId.description: Reference to the User (seller) who managed or created this order. (Relationship: User 1:N Order)

origin.description: Indicates if the order originated from the web quoter ('quoter') or was created manually by a seller ('manual').

productTypeId.description: Reference to the ProductType for this order (e.g., 'T-shirt'). (Relationship: ProductType 1:N Order)

quantity.description: Number of units of the product ordered for embroidery.

productColor.description: Color of the product to be embroidered (e.g., '#000000' or 'Negro').

logoId.description: Reference to the Logo used for this order. (Relationship: Logo 1:N Order)

embroideryPositionId.description: Reference to the EmbroideryPosition chosen for this order (e.g., 'Chest'). (Relationship: EmbroideryPosition 1:N Order)

notes.description: Additional notes or special instructions for the order, from the customer or internal staff.

deadline.description: The target date and time by which the order must be completed and ready for pickup/delivery.

status.description: Current status of the order in the production pipeline.

assignedMachineId.description: Reference to the Machine assigned to process this order. Can be null if not yet assigned. (Relationship: Machine 1:N Order)

assignedOperatorId.description: Reference to the User (operator role) assigned to work on this order. Can be null if not yet assigned. (Relationship: User 1:N Order)

estimatedProductionTimeHours.description: Estimated time in hours required for the embroidery production of this order.

actualProductionStartTime.description: Timestamp when an operator started working on the order. Null if production has not begun.

actualProductionEndTime.description: Timestamp when an operator marked the order as completed. Null if not yet finished.

totalAmount.description: The final total price charged to the customer for the order.

amountPaid.description: The amount of money already paid by the customer towards this order.

balanceDue.description: The remaining balance the customer owes for this order (totalAmount - amountPaid).

alertSent.description: Flag indicating if a deadline reminder alert has been sent for this order, typically by an automation system.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the order was initially created in the system.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the order's details were last updated.

OrderStatusHistory.title: OrderStatusHistory
OrderStatusHistory.description: Records the historical changes in the status of an order, providing an audit trail of its progression.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderStatusHistory entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the Order whose status was changed. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderStatusHistory)

oldStatus.description: The status of the order before the change occurred.

newStatus.description: The new status of the order after the change.

changedByUserId.description: Reference to the User who performed the status change. (Relationship: User 1:N OrderStatusHistory)

changeTimestamp.description: Timestamp when the status change occurred.

PaymentTransaction.title: PaymentTransaction
PaymentTransaction.description: Records individual payment transactions made by customers for their orders.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PaymentTransaction entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the Order to which this payment applies. (Relationship: Order 1:N PaymentTransaction)

customerId.description: Reference to the Customer who made the payment. (Relationship: Customer 1:N PaymentTransaction)

amount.description: The amount of money paid in this transaction.

transactionDate.description: Date and time when the payment was processed.

notes.description: Optional notes or comments related to the payment transaction.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the payment transaction record was created.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Access is path-based (user can read/write their own profile) and role-based for admins.
Collection for DBAC. Existence of a document here indicates the user has 'admin' role. Used for authorization checks.
Collection for DBAC. Existence of a document here indicates the user has 'seller' role. Used for authorization checks.
Collection for DBAC. Existence of a document here indicates the user has 'operator' role. Used for authorization checks.
Stores customer leads from the web quoter. Includes denormalized 'sellerId' for authorization independence. Sellers can only list their own leads, admins can list all.
Stores confirmed customer information. Accessible by sellers and admins.
Customer-uploaded logos. Path-based ownership (`customerId`) ensures authorization independence. Accessible by the customer, their assigned seller, and admins.
Pre-defined example logos for public use. Read-only for all, managed by admins. Segregates public data from customer-specific data.
Defines product types available for embroidery. Read-only for public and all users, managed by admins. Homogeneous security posture.
Defines embroidery positions. Read-only for public and all users, managed by admins. Homogeneous security posture.
Stores industrial embroidery machine information. Operators can update status, admins manage all fields, sellers can read.

Main collection for customer orders. Includes denormalized 'customerId', 'sellerId', and 'assignedOperatorId' for authorization independence. Sellers can list their own orders, operators their assigned orders, and admins all orders.

Audit trail for order status changes. Includes denormalized 'orderSellerId' and 'orderAssignedOperatorId' for authorization independence, allowing access based on parent order's authorized users without `get()` calls.

Records individual payment transactions for an order. Includes denormalized 'orderSellerId' and 'orderCustomerId' for authorization independence, allowing access based on parent order's authorized users without `get()` calls.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed following the core principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and consistent Access Modeling to ensure security, scalability, and debuggability. 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
This is the paramount principle applied across the design. To eliminate problematic `get()` calls in security rules and enable atomic operations, key authorization attributes are denormalized into relevant documents:

1.  **`CustomerLead`**: The `sellerId` is directly included in each `customer_lead` document. This allows a seller to directly check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.sellerId` without needing to fetch any parent or related user document.
2.  **`Order`**: Each `order` document includes `customerId`, `sellerId`, and `assignedOperatorId`. This allows authorization rules to directly check `request.auth.uid` against these fields for sellers and operators, and potentially `customerId` if customer-facing access were to be added. Admins are granted access via their global role.
3.  **`OrderStatusHistory`**: Each `status_history` document within an order's subcollection includes `orderId`, `orderSellerId`, and `orderAssignedOperatorId`. This denormalization ensures that rules for status history do not need to `get()` the parent order document for authorization checks, making them atomic and independent.
4.  **`PaymentTransaction`**: Similarly, each `payment_transaction` document within an order's subcollection includes `orderId`, `orderSellerId`, and `orderCustomerId`. This allows authorization rules for payments to directly verify `request.auth.uid` against the relevant seller or customer ID, without relying on parent `order` data.
5.  **`Logo`**: Customer-specific logos are stored in a subcollection under the customer path (`/customers/{customerId}/logos/{logoId}`). This path-based ownership inherently denormalizes `customerId` into the document's location, allowing direct authorization `request.auth.uid == customerId`.

**Support for QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
Query-based Access Patterns (QAPs) are crucial for secure `list` operations. The structure supports this through:

1.  **Structural Segregation**: Collections like `/product_types` and `/embroidery_positions` are designed for public or broad read access, making `list` operations simple and secure (e.g., `allow read: if true;`). The `/example_logos` collection also falls into this category.
2.  **Role-Based Access**: Global roles are managed via dedicated collections (e.g., `/roles_admin/{uid}`). Presence of a document in these collections grants a specific role, enabling efficient `exists()` checks in rules (e.g., `allow list: if exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))`).
3.  **Attribute-Based Filtering for `list`**: For data like `customer_leads` and `orders`, the denormalized `sellerId` and `assignedOperatorId` fields are designed to be used in Firestore queries (e.g., `db.collection('orders').where('sellerId', '==', currentUser.uid)`). Security rules would then validate that any `list` request for sellers/operators includes these specific `where` clauses, preventing unauthorized access to other users' data. For instance, a seller's rule might be `allow list: if request.auth.uid == resource.data.sellerId && request.query.where.sellerId == request.auth.uid;` (simplified for illustration, actual rules would be more robust). This ensures that the client-side query is enforced by the rules, guaranteeing that only permitted documents are returned.

**Structural Segregation and Access Modeling:**

*   **Private Data**: User profiles (`/users/{userId}`) are path-based for individual ownership.
*   **User-Owned Data (1:N)**: `/customers/{customerId}/logos/{logoId}` uses a hierarchical path to link logos directly to their customer owners, simplifying authorization.
*   **Global Roles (DBAC)**: `/roles_admin/{uid}`, `/roles_seller/{uid}`, `/roles_operator/{uid}` collections grant roles based on document existence.
*   **Homogeneous Security Posture**: Each collection (or subcollection) is designed such that all documents within it share the same basic security requirements. For example, all documents in `/product_types` are globally readable, while all documents in `/orders/{orderId}/status_history` are accessible based on the parent order's authorized users (seller, operator, admin) via denormalized fields.

This architecture prioritizes clear, secure, and performant data access, minimizing rule complexity and maximizing debuggability.
